articles.title: How much money did Jon Jones really leave on the table?
articles.description: Get $10 Off at BRUNT with code CHAEL at https://www.bruntworkwear.com/CHAEL #BruntpodChael Chael Sonnen breaks down one of the biggest financial what-ifs in UFC history. From sitting out three full years between light heavyweight and heavyweight, to missing massive paydays during suspensions, the numbers start stacking up fast. Conservative estimates alone suggest tens of millions in missed earnings—and that’s before even factoring in career momentum and timing. But this isn’t just about Jon Jones. It’s about a choice every fighter faces: chase one massive payday… or keep showing up and stacking smaller ones. Chael compares it to baseball—hit enough singles, and you’re in the Hall of Fame. Wait for the home run, and you might never swing again. The deeper question is about time. Fighters don’t know when their last fight is coming. And when the window closes, so does the opportunity to earn. Whether Jones made the right call depends on how you value legacy, longevity, and risk—but the financial tradeoff is impossible to ignore. Watch more breakdowns on fight business, strategy, and what really happens behind the scenes. #JonJones #ChaelSonnen #UFC #MMA #FightBusiness #Money #Heavyweight #UFC200 0:00 – How much money has Jon Jones left on the table? 0:45 – The $18 million calculation: 3 years and 9 fights 2:00 – The high cost of suspensions and UFC 200 2:50 – The philosophy of "hitting singles" in the UFC 4:10 – Bullets in the chamber: You don't know when it's over 5:30 – Why Jon Jones realistically has 3 fights left Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Dillon Danis Proved Us Wrong? The RAF 07 Breakdown.
articles.description: Real American Freestyle 07 just went down in Tampa, and the headlines are writing themselves. Bo Bassett didn't just beat an Olympic champion in Vladimer Khinchegashvili; he finished him 13-3 with time to spare. We saw Colby Covington handle Dillon Danis before calling out Chris Weidman for a showdown. Meanwhile, Arman Tsarukyan proved he’s the most dangerous man without a belt, taking out Georgio Poullas and confirming his status as the UFC’s premier backup fighter. From Kyle Snyder finally getting his win over Taz to Kyle Dake defying history against Keckeisen, the mats were on fire. This isn't just about scores; it’s about the shift in power. When a high school senior like Bo Bassett dismantles an Olympic gold medalist, the rules of combat change. Whether it’s the brewing rivalry between Bassett and Jax Forrest or Tsarukyan’s march toward a UFC title, you are looking at the future of elite competition. Make sure you’re subscribed so you don’t miss the next chapter of this saga. #BoBassett #ChaelSonnen #RAF07 #ColbyCovington #ArmanTsarukyan #Wrestling #UFC #KyleSnyder #UFC #MMA #Wrestling #ChaelSonnen #RAF7 #ArmanTsarukyan #bobasset 0:00 – The Real American Freestyle Recap 0:20 – Bo Bassett’s Incredible 13-3 Victory 2:32 – The Potential Bassett vs. Jax Forrest Super-Match 3:48 – Colby Covington vs. Dillon Danis Breakdown 4:25 – Colby Calls Out Chris Weidman 4:49 – Arman Tsarukyan’s Dominance & UFC Backup Status 8:10 – Kyle Snyder vs. Akhmed Tazhudinov Result 8:22 – Kyle Dake Defies History Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Jake Paul Says Ngannou Can’t Box?! #ufc
articles.description: Hold on… we’re saying Francis Ngannou can’t box? That’s hard to believe after what we all just saw. He went in there with Tyson Fury, dropped him, and arguably won the fight. That wasn’t hype. That wasn’t luck. That was real skill under the brightest lights in combat sports. Now Jake Paul is saying Francis couldn’t box and even claims Francis avoided him in sparring. Maybe that happened. Maybe it didn’t. But once you perform like Ngannou did against Fury, the conversation changes. You don’t get dismissed as someone who “can’t box.” You just don’t. Now sure — that fight would be massive. Huge numbers. Huge attention. Two crossover stars colliding. But here’s the bigger question nobody’s asking: Why? It’s not money — Jake has it. It’s not fame — he’s already there. So what’s the real goal? Proof of courage. And here’s the reality: Jake’s already proved that. You don’t step into boxing, take real shots, headline events, and stay active without guts. That box is checked. So this fight with Ngannou? It’s not about proving bravery anymore — it’s about risk. Because Ngannou isn’t a spectacle after Fury. He’s a problem. And that makes this a very different conversation. #JakePaul #FrancisNgannou #TysonFury #Boxing #MMA #CombatSports #FightTalk #Heavyweight #CrossoverFight #FightNews

articles.title: “This Was My Dream!” DJ Comments Hall Of Fame Introduction
Expectations Ruined The White House Card #ufc
articles.description: Joe Rogan called the White House card a “gimmick.” Not as an insult — but let’s be honest, that word carries weight. And I don’t see it. Yes, the location is unique. Fighting at the White House? That’s different. There were even wild ideas floating around about using the Oval Office as a dressing room. Sure, that’s a little gimmicky. But the card itself? That’s where the argument falls apart. Take the venue away. Strip the theme. Now just look at the fights: Ilia Topuria vs Justin Gaethje. Alex Pereira moving to heavyweight against Ciryl Gane. Winner likely facing Tom Aspinall. If I told you that lineup with no “White House” attached… You’d lose your mind. That’s a stacked card. What happened here is simple: expectations got out of control. Once you attach a historic venue, people expect something impossible — five title fights, every superstar, a once-in-a-lifetime spectacle. And when reality shows up with a great card instead of a perfect one? They call it a gimmick. But fights still matter. Matchups still matter. Stakes still matter. And this card has all three. The venue is a bonus. The fights are the story. And if you judge it on that? This is anything but a gimmick. #UFC #MMA #JoeRogan #IliaTopuria #JustinGaethje #AlexPereira #CirylGane #TomAspinall #FightCard #CombatSports

articles.title: Daniel Cormier REACTS to Joe Pyfer TKO vs. Israel Adesanya, Alexa Grasso INSANE KO vs. Maycee Barber
articles.description: Israel Adesanya got FINISHING by Joe Pyfer in an incredible night of UFC fights from Seattle, and Daniel Cormier IS BACK with another post-fight reaction live from the octagon! DC reacts to Izzy's TKO loss to Pyfer and BELIEVES Adesanya when he says he's not done with the sport of MMA and his career in the UFC. Plus, DC gives his take on Pyfer's win and why this victory gets him straight into the championship conversation. And don't miss Cormier discuss Alexa Grasso's CRAZY knockout and submission of Maycee Barber and what it was like to witness that in person. DC says that Grasso's finish was not only the finish of the year, but one of the most impressive finishes he's EVER SEEN in his time in the UFC. Chael Predicts Conor McGregor’s Comeback Fight
articles.description: Who should Conor McGregor fight in his return—and does it even matter? In this episode of Ask Uncle Chael, Chael Sonnen answers fan questions ranging from Conor McGregor’s comeback opponent to the real key in Sean Strickland vs Khamzat Chimaev. Chael explains why McGregor is a rare draw where the opponent almost doesn’t matter—and even predicts a potential matchup with Ian Garry. He also breaks down why Strickland vs Chimaev may be decided in the very first round, and what that tells us about both fighters. Beyond the matchups, Chael reflects on his own career, revealing the one trait that gave him opportunities others never got—and what he would (and wouldn’t) change. From wild hypotheticals to real fight analysis, this is Chael at his best: direct, unpredictable, and always entertaining. #UFC #ConorMcGregor #ChaelSonnen #MMA 0:00 Ask Uncle Chael Begins 0:03 Conor McGregor Return 0:35 Why Opponent Doesn’t Matter 1:03 Best Self-Defense Advice 1:11 Strickland vs Chimaev Odds 1:52 First Round Decides Everything 2:30 Career Reflection 3:33 Why Chael Got Opportunities 3:55 Fixing the Welterweight Division 4:42 Why Fights Need Personal Grudge 5:07 Ian Garry’s Value 6:40 White House Card Reaction 7:33 Why Fans Are Wrong About It 8:29 Behind-the-Scenes Updates 9:20 D.B. articles.title: Hey Jon Jones, let’s play WOULD YOU RATHER.
articles.description: Did Jon Jones really turn down $15 million… or did he make a much bigger mistake? Chael Sonnen breaks down the real story behind Jon Jones’ decision and why this isn’t about money—it’s about risk, control, and a misunderstanding that even smart fighters fall into. Using a simple “Would You Rather” scenario, Chael explains the difference between guaranteed money and chasing a percentage of something that may never materialize. It’s a lesson that applies far beyond fighting—and one that could define the final chapter of Jon Jones’ career. From the rumored White House fight to the risks of waiting for the “perfect deal,” Chael lays out why walking away from a sure thing can cost more than just one payday. This isn’t about Jon taking less. It’s about Jon choosing nothing. #UFC #JonJones #ChaelSonnen #MMA 0:28 $1M vs 80% Explained 1:03 Why Emotion Kills Logic 2:02 Risk = Reward 2:21 Eddie Hearn’s 80% Model 3:04 The Hidden Risk of Percentages 3:57 Jon Jones & $15M Offer 4:26 Why Opponent Matters 5:07 Jones Chooses Release 5:27 He Didn’t Take Less—He Took Zero 5:38 The Andre Ward Example 6:43 Time Is the Real Cost 7:02 The $18M Missed Opportunity 7:34 Final Question: Risk or Guarantee Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Tsarukyan’s Brutal Admission #ufc
articles.description: I just spent the day with Arman Tsarukyan — and what he told me caught me off guard. No excuses. No blame. No spin. When I asked about the incident with his opponent, I expected the usual answer — “he started it,” “something was said,” “things got heated.” Instead? Arman shut it down immediately. “He did nothing.” No trash talk. No cheap shot. No trigger. Then he went further. He said it was his plan all along. Wait until the match ended… and then strike. A punch. A knee to the body. Planned. That level of honesty is rare in fight sports. Most guys rewrite the story. They protect themselves. They shift the narrative. Arman didn’t. He owned it completely. And whether you agree with it or not, that kind of transparency forces a different conversation: Is it gamesmanship? Is it crossing a line? Or is it just raw competitiveness taken too far? Because in a sport built on intensity, emotion, and control, that moment sits right on the edge of all three. Arman didn’t justify it. He didn’t deny it. He told it exactly how it happened. And now it’s on everyone else to decide what it means. Respect the honesty — or question the action. But either way, the story just changed. #UFC #MMA #ArmanTsarukyan #FightNews #CombatSports #Wrestling #MMADrama #FightTalk #AthleteMindset

articles.title: McGregor Return Just Got Real #ufc
articles.description: I’m telling you — Conor McGregor is coming back. And not someday… soon. For a long time, the plan looked simple: Conor vs Michael Chandler. But here’s the problem — time. Over two years have passed. And when that much time goes by, you don’t just pick up where you left off. You reset. You go back to step one. That’s where things get complicated. Chandler still makes sense on paper — action, name value, guaranteed fireworks. But there’s a real question now: how much does he have left? How many miles are on that engine? So the conversation opens up. A fresh matchup like Ian Machado Garry starts gaining traction. New blood. Clean timing. A striking-heavy fight that tests Conor without throwing him straight into chaos. Other options? Less likely. Title fights? Doesn’t seem realistic. Big jumps in weight? Probably not happening. Legacy fights outside the division? Not right now. This is about getting Conor back in the cage — and doing it the right way. Because history tells us: You don’t return a superstar after years away and throw him into the deep end immediately. You build it. Step by step. But make no mistake — the return is real. The opponent? That’s the question. And the clock is ticking. #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #MichaelChandler #IanGarry #UFCReturn #FightNews #CombatSports #MMARumors

articles.title: Eddie Hearn Is Fighting the Wrong Battle
articles.description: Chael Sonnen explains why Eddie Hearn entering MMA is a losing strategy and why the real battle isn’t where he thinks it is. Using examples like Mark Cuban stepping back after exploring MMA promotion and the dominance of Dana White, Chael breaks down what actually makes a fight promotion work, and why simply having talent or resources isn’t enough. From the lack of structure in combat sports to the realities of building a roster and running events consistently, this is a deeper look at why MMA isn’t as easy to enter as it seems. The message is clear: if Eddie wants to win, he needs to fight Dana in boxing, not follow him into MMA. #ufc #EddieHearn #DanaWhite #MMA #ChaelSonnen 0:00 Eddie Hearn Enters MMA 0:20 Chael’s Warning 0:50 The Mark Cuban Lesson 1:30 Why He Walked Away 2:00 The Real Mistake 2:30 Dana vs Eddie Explained 3:00 Why MMA Isn’t Easy 3:30 One Event vs Many 4:00 The Roster Problem 4:30 “Best Fighters” Myth 5:00 No Real Structure 5:30 The Media Game 6:00 Where Eddie Loses 6:30 The Fight He Should Take 6:50 Final Verdict Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: UFC Didn’t Need Jon Jones?s #ufc
articles.description: This is where things get interesting. Jon Jones says he was ready. Willing. Able. Even willing to take less money — around $15 million — to make the fight happen. And still… it didn’t happen. According to Jones, he was prepared to face Alex Pereira instead of waiting on Tom Aspinall. Different opponent, different path — same goal: get back in the cage. But the UFC didn’t go that direction. Now you’ve got two versions of the story: Jones says he was in. The UFC says otherwise. Somebody’s not lining up with reality. But here’s the part that actually matters: The UFC moved on without him. That’s the headline. Because for years, Jon Jones was the center of everything — the biggest name, the biggest draw, the guy you had to build around. Now? The machine kept going. New stars. New matchups. New plans. And no Jones required. That doesn’t mean he’s not great. It doesn’t mean he’s not still dangerous. It means the promotion proved something: No one is bigger than the schedule. Fights will happen. Belts will move. Cards will get built. With or without you. That’s the reality of the fight business. And for the first time, Jon Jones might be on the outside looking in. #UFC #MMA #JonJones #AlexPereira #TomAspinall #DanaWhite #FightNews #MMABusiness #CombatSports

articles.title: Dana’s “Slip” Changes Everything #ufc
articles.description: Did Dana White just let it slip? There’s growing belief that Conor McGregor vs Ian Machado Garry is being lined up — and not everyone thinks it was an accident. The clue? Dana talking about returning to Dublin… but making it clear that Conor vs Garry wouldn’t happen in Ireland. That’s not a denial. That’s a redirect. Some are calling it a Freudian slip — the kind where the truth sneaks out before it’s supposed to. Because if the fight wasn’t real, why even separate the location from the matchup? And stylistically? It’s fascinating. Two strikers. Two personalities. Two very different stages of their careers. Garry has openly shown admiration for McGregor. There’s respect there — but also opportunity. A win over Conor changes everything for a rising contender. For McGregor, it’s a different equation: Return fight. Timing test. Sharp, technical opponent. No easy tune-up. This wouldn’t be nostalgia. This would be real risk. And that’s what makes it compelling. The UFC loves matchups that blend star power with stakes — and this checks both boxes. Nothing official yet. But sometimes, the truth shows itself early. And if this fight does get announced? It might’ve already been revealed. #UFC #MMA #ConorMcGregor #IanGarry #DanaWhite #FightNews #MMARumors #CombatSports #UFCReturn
